description_long: This is a segment of barbed wire fence, featuring two sturdy vertical wooden posts at each end and a central span of coiled barbed wire running horizontally between them. The barbed wire is arranged in a helical pattern, with sharp barbs protruding at regular intervals along the wire, designed to deter passage. The wooden posts are weathered, suggesting outdoor use, and are firmly anchored to support the tension of the wire. The overall construction is utilitarian, prioritizing security and boundary marking over aesthetics. The wire is metallic, likely galvanized steel, and the posts are made of solid wood. This type of fence is commonly used in urban and rural environments to restrict access or protect property.
description: A barbed wire fence segment with two wooden posts and a helical coil of sharp barbed wire stretched between them. The design is utilitarian and intended for security or boundary marking.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the fence, with both wooden posts and the helical barbed wire clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the circular profile of the coiled barbed wire and the thickness of one wooden post.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the barbed wire's helical pattern and both posts from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the circular coil of the barbed wire and the profile of the wooden post.
